title: [phase:plan] [family:approval-tokens] [provider_hint:codex] Current task: openclaw-tmux replacement. Review the new prototype and propose the minimum scoped approval-token model to replace coarse --approve. Return concise bullets: token fields, validation rules, TTL, and how to bind token to action+...
summary_text: - Resolution precedence (strict-first): - `NOTEBOOKLM_READINESS_NLM_BIN` (explicit per-run override, highest). - `FUGUE_NOTEBOOKLM_BIN` (repo/org Actions variable, stable runner default). - `NLM_BIN` (process env fallback). - `PATH` lookup (`command -v <name>`), default name `nlm`. - Deterministic common absolute candidates (in order): `/opt/homebrew/bin/nlm`, `/usr/local/bin/nlm`, `/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/nlm`, `$HOME/.npm-global/bin/nlm`, `$HOME/.local/bin/nlm`. - Validation after resolution: - Must resolve to a single executable file. - Must pass non-destructive probe (`<bin> --help` exit 0). - Use the same resolved binary path for readiness gate and preflight (`NLM_BIN`) to avoid split-brain behavior. - Still fail closed: - No binary found after full precedence chain. - Resolved binary is non-executable or probe fails. - Any attempt to “auto-install”/`npx` download during gate (disallow). - `LIVE_SMOKE_MODE=required` / `EXECUTE_LIVE_MODE=required` failures remain hard-stop (no downgrade).
